subject: Public Health
title: Plague's Return and Quarantine's Birth
description: A quick win: understand Plague's Return and Quarantine's Birth.

hook.text: Plague didn't strike once—it returned every generation for 300 years across Europe.

buildup.text: The Great Plague of London in 1665 killed 100,000 people—a quarter of the city's population.

discovery.text: Venice invented formal quarantine in 1377—holding ships at anchor for 40 days before docking.

twist.text: Quarantine worked without anyone understanding the science—pure observation and pattern recognition.

climax.text: The practice spread across Mediterranean ports and became the foundation of epidemic control.

punchline.text: Quarantine was invented by merchants, not doctors.

quiz.question: Where was formal quarantine first invented?
